layman_headline: Ethereum shows early signs of a rebound while the broader market is still fearful.
layman_explanation: The crypto market has been in a fearful state, but sentiment is improving — the Fear & Greed index jumped from 25 to 33 in just one day. Ethereum has printed three strong green candles in a row (called 'Three White Soldiers'), which historically signals the start of upward momentum. BTC is also recovering, up nearly 3% this week, which gives ETH a supportive backdrop. With ETH sitting near $1,900 support and resistance at $2,000 not far above, this is a clean setup with defined risk.
layman_action: Consider a small position in Ethereum near $1,920 with a stop-loss at $1,870. If it breaks above $2,000, momentum could carry it to $2,050+. Exit if it drops below $1,870.
expert_reasoning: ETH presents the highest-quality swing setup among the candidates. The Three White Soldiers pattern on the chart is a strong bullish reversal signal with 5-day momentum of +19.7% from what was a sustained downtrend. RSI at 43.85 is neutral — critically, not overbought — meaning there's room to run before hitting resistance. The $1,900 support is only 1.4% below current price while $2,000 resistance is 3.8% above, yielding a 2.84x risk/reward ratio. The improving Fear & Greed trend (25→33, week avg 27.4) suggests the market is transitioning from capitulation to recovery, and ETH with $41.3B in DeFi TVL remains the most fundamentally anchored altcoin. BTC dominance at 56.8% is a headwind for alts, but ETH tends to lead alt recovery when BTC stabilizes.
risk_factors: BTC faces key resistance at $68,000 per CoinDesk — rejection there could drag ETH back below $1,900 support
BTC dominance at 56.8% is still rising, meaning capital rotation into alts hasn't truly begun yet
Fear & Greed at 33 is improving but still in 'Fear' — sentiment could reverse on any macro shock or regulatory setback with the CLARITY Act
watch_list: BTC price action at $68,000 resistance — a breakout would be very bullish for ETH, a rejection could trigger selling
ETH reaction at $2,000 psychological resistance — does it break through or get rejected?
Fear & Greed index trajectory — continued improvement toward 40+ would confirm risk-on rotation into alts
market_context: The crypto market is recovering from deep fear (F&G improving from 25 to 33), with BTC up 2.7% on the week and testing key resistance near $68K. High BTC dominance at 56.8% suggests alt season hasn't started yet, but improving sentiment could trigger early rotation into large-cap alts like ETH.
context_banner: Ethereum prints a bullish Three White Soldiers reversal pattern with a 2.84x risk/reward setup as market fear begins to thaw.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 43.85 is neutral with room to run higher — not overbought, confirming this isn't a chasing entry.
Three White Soldiers candlestick pattern on ETH signals sustained buying pressure and a likely uptrend acceleration from the $1,900 level.
Fear & Greed jumped from 25 to 33 in one day with a week average of 27.4 — improving trend from deep fear historically precedes bounce rallies.
ETH breaking $1,900 resistance reported as a bullish breakout with $2,100 as the next target — provides narrative catalyst for momentum traders.
BTC rally facing key test at $68K — outcome will determine whether ETH gets tailwind support or faces pullback risk.
